You ask "How does reason persuade, overrule [ak] Ananke?" I can tell you: I, peronally, experienced it. The true (i.e. "Felix") message had to be carefully hidden because - I guess because of our occlusion. Who was the message for? Anyone who could be subliminally cued in? Like a proclamation - yes, the good news for all whom it could be gotten to.

Thus the need to smuggle in the kerygma, as if originally way, before Paul got hold of it. Before the moralists, the puritans, got in. Time must be rolled back to Pentecost, Paul represents the transistion; he was + was not a genuine Xtian.

We must go back to the disciples then the feast of agapē, + the Lord (Holy Spirit) descending on them; i.e. Jesus rejoining them as before. Just as I experienced Zebra, + celebrated the feast of agape with [Christogher] time had been rolled back.

In "Tears" in the dream "Jason Taverner" shut up in the house crying out "one single shriek" is Christ on the cross crying out just before he dies; i.e. just before they kill him, by God's command - God as the white haired old king. Christ has taken upon himself the sins of the world; hence he is "shut up in darkness." The dream is of the crucifixion, which took place at God's command. But we know Jason -i.e. Jesus- is innocent, + so he is seen to be later in "Tears.
